mappings.rationale: Covers the study of disease distribution and determinants, directly relevant to detecting, diagnosing, and classifying human diseases.
Directly covers infectious and contagious diseases (section D.5), including the impairment of health by living invaders of the body.
Covers immunological disorders, allergenic diseases (section D.11), and biological therapy including immune-based treatments (section C.2.b).
Covers viral agents responsible for many infectious diseases including childhood diseases and contagious illnesses (sections D.5, D.7).
Covers bacterial pathogens causing infectious diseases and antibiotic treatments, relevant to chemotherapy and pharmacodynamic therapy (sections C.2.c, C.2.d).
Directly covers pharmacodynamic therapy and chemotherapy (sections C.2.c, C.2.d) as major therapeutic techniques for treating disease.
Directly covers tumours and cancers (section E), including diagnosis and treatment including radiation therapy, chemotherapy, and surgical treatment.
Covers a major infectious disease that is also a sexually transmitted disease (section F.6.b), including its diagnosis and treatment.
Covers epidemic and pandemic infectious diseases, relevant to contagious diseases (section D.5) and their global impact on human health.
Covers a major historic infectious disease, relevant to understanding contagious diseases and epidemics (section D.5).
Covers mental and neurological disorders, relevant to diseases of the nervous system (section F.9) and psychological therapy (section C.2.l).
Covers a specific neurological/psychiatric disorder relevant to disorders of the central nervous system (section F.9.d).
Covers a major mental/neurological disorder, relevant to disorders of the general nervous system (section F.9.e).
Covers a neurodevelopmental disorder relevant to diseases and disorders present at birth and childhood diseases (sections D.6, D.7).
Covers anxiety disorders, a category of mental disorders related to nervous system dysfunction (section F.9).
Covers degenerative disorders of the central nervous system (section F.9.d), including diagnosis and treatment approaches.
Covers substance use disorders relevant to poisoning (section D.10) and nervous system disorders (section F.9).
Provides historical context for the development of disease detection, diagnosis, and therapeutic techniques described in sections B and C.
Covers ethical considerations in therapeutic decision-making, relevant to formulating therapeutic regimens (section C.1).
Covers legal dimensions of disease treatment, relevant to aspects of medical treatment and therapeutic regimen formulation (section C.1).
Relevant to disease detection and diagnosis (section B) and identification of causes of death including poisoning and injury.
Directly relevant to diseases of the blood and blood-forming tissues (section F.1.d) and disorders of blood circulation (section F.1.c).
Directly relevant to diseases of the endocrine system (section F.5), including pituitary, thyroid, adrenal, and pancreatic disorders.
Directly covers nutritional diseases related to deficiencies and excesses (section D.3) and disorders in digestion and absorption of foods (section F.4.g).
Directly relevant to surgical treatment (section C.2.a) as a critical component of modern surgical therapeutic techniques.
Relevant to disorders of the central nervous system (section F.9.d) and neurological manifestations of disease.
Relevant to disorders of the eye and vision (section F.9.f).
Fungal pathogens cause infectious diseases (section D.5), including skin diseases (section F.8.g) and respiratory infections (section F.3.a).
Covers the microbial agents responsible for infectious diseases and the microbiological basis for diagnosis and treatment.
Directly covers psychological therapy (section C.2.l) as a major therapeutic technique for treating disease.
A specific form of psychological therapy (section C.2.l) used to treat anxiety disorders, depression, and other mental disorders.
Relevant to radiation therapy (section C.2.f) and disorders caused by radiation exposure (section D.8).
A neurodevelopmental disorder relevant to disorders of speech and cognitive function (section F.9.i).
Covers personality disorders relevant to the section's coverage of disorders of the nervous system and mental health (section F.9).
Relevant to diseases of the reproductive system (section F.6) including sexually transmitted diseases (section F.6.b).
Relevant to age-related diseases and metabolic disorders (section D.2) as well as degenerative conditions across body systems.
Relevant to disorders caused by environmental physical agents and exposure to temperature extremes (section D.8).
